title: lathyrus vestitus subsp. puberulus
intro: Lathyrus vestitus subsp. puberulus, also known as the hairy flowering pea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic effects. The plant is rich in nutrients and offers various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting kidney function. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus vestitus subsp. puberulus can be made by drying the plant's leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for medicinal use.
